meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome testing anxiety. An Intermediate-level tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Testing anxiety often stems from complex architectures. By implementing SaaS pricing models first, you create a modular, revenue-focused framework. Using Antigravity to scaffold your subscription logic and Claude Code to automate unit testing ensures your code is production-ready, turning your fear of bugs into a robust, scalable feature set.
content.intro:
You have the vision, the stack, and the drive, but a silent wall stands between you and your first production-ready SaaS: the terrifying void of testing. You see experienced engineers writing unit tests with ease, while you stare at your code, paralyzed by the fear that one deployment could break everything. This isn't just about syntax; it's about the confidence to ship.

We are going to bridge that gap by building a sophisticated SaaS pricing engine. By treating your pricing logic as a testable, modular service, we remove the guesswork from your development cycle. With the right tools—Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for automated verification—you won't just learn to test; you will learn to build with the unshakeable confidence of a senior developer.

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Model
steps.description: Define your pricing tiers and subscription logic before writing a single line of functional code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable TypeScript structure for a SaaS pricing service including Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers with period-based billing logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate pricing service boilerplate with TypeScript interfaces'

steps.title: Implementing Tiered Logic
steps.description: Map your business rules to code, ensuring the logic is isolated and easily testable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a pricing calculator function that accepts user tier and usage data, returning calculated costs based on pre-defined constants.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement pricing calculator logic in src/services/pricing.ts'

steps.title: Automating Test Scenarios
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate unit tests that cover edge cases in your pricing engine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest test cases for the pricing calculator, covering tier upgrades, downgrades, and invalid usage inputs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create unit tests for pricing.ts using Jest'

steps.title: Integration with Payment Gateways
steps.description: Connect your validated logic to real-world infrastructure using secure patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an adapter pattern for Stripe integration that validates pricing calculations against the local service before proceeding to checkout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement Stripe adapter pattern with validation checks'

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Validation
steps.description: Automate your testing flow so that your code is verified every time you push to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the pricing test suite and blocks deployment if any test fails.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure .github/workflows/test.yml to run Jest on push'

pro_tips: Always TDD your pricing logic: if you can't write a test for it, your pricing model is too complex for a MVP.
Use mock objects for Stripe API responses in your tests to avoid hitting live billing endpoints during development.
Implement 'Feature Flags' for your pricing tiers; this allows you to toggle features without redeploying your entire application.
Log every pricing calculation in development mode to identify rounding errors before they reach your production database.
Treat your tests as documentation; if a future developer needs to know how pricing works, they should read your test file, not the source.
content.next_steps: Once you have verified your pricing logic, integrate a real database like Supabase to persist user subscriptions, then deploy your first MVP to Vercel.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with the tools to ship production-grade software. The gap between your idea and a profitable SaaS has just vanished. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
